description: PRD-0 through PRD-9: Complete TaxEase Nigeria application build

PRD-0.name: Foundation — Auth, Onboarding & Entity Setup
PRD-0.overview: Establishes the foundational layer for TaxEase Nigeria — auth flow, onboarding wizard, app shell with side drawer navigation, profile management, entity management, and preferences. Without this, no other PRD can function.
PRD-0.background: TaxEase Nigeria is a tax compliance app for Nigerian freelancers and SMEs. PRD-0 is the root dependency — it delivers Clerk-based authentication, a 4-step onboarding wizard (user type, info, NIN/TIN, connect accounts), multi-entity support, and the empty app shell. The existing codebase has basic Clerk auth, a users/profiles/transactions schema, and simple pages — but needs a complete overhaul to match this PRD.
goals: Complete auth flow: email+password, Google OAuth, password reset via Clerk
4-step onboarding wizard that creates user profile and first tax entity
Multi-entity support with CRUD, switching, and soft-delete
App shell with side drawer navigation and entity selector
Profile management with NIN masking and avatar upload
User preferences for notifications and reminders
Category seed data for transaction classification
Logout and account deletion with data cleanup

coreConcepts.Entity: A tax entity (individual, business_name, or llc) for which a tax return is filed. Users can have multiple entities and switch between them.
coreConcepts.Onboarding: 4-step wizard: (1) User type, (2) Personal/business info + first entity creation, (3) NIN/TIN, (4) Connect accounts or skip. Sets onboardingComplete=true on finish.
coreConcepts.NIN: National Identification Number — 11 digits, serves as Tax ID under NTA 2025. Encrypted at rest.
coreConcepts.Category: Transaction category (income, business_expense, personal_expense, transfer). System categories are seeded; users can create custom ones later.

PRD-1.name: Transaction Management & Import Pipeline
PRD-1.overview: Delivers the data backbone of TaxEase: import, view, categorise, and manage financial transactions. Transactions are the raw material for tax calculations, dashboards, reports, and filing.
PRD-1.background: Users need to import bank statements (PDF/CSV), manually add transactions, view filterable/sortable lists, categorise via triage UI, edit tax fields (WHT, deductible %), and manage custom categories. The import pipeline is source-agnostic so PRD-8 (Bank Linking) can feed the same batchUpsert path later.
goals: Transaction schema with all tax-relevant fields (whtDeducted, deductiblePercent, amountNgn, etc.)
PDF/CSV import with parsing, dedup, and import job tracking
Filterable, sortable, searchable, paginated transaction list
Transaction detail/edit with all tax fields
Categorisation triage UI for rapid uncategorised review
Bulk select, categorise, and delete
Custom category CRUD
Connected accounts view and add (upload-only in v1)

coreConcepts.ImportJob: Tracks a file import lifecycle: pending → processing → complete/failed. Records totalParsed, totalImported, duplicatesSkipped.
coreConcepts.batchUpsert: Source-agnostic mutation that writes parsed transactions with dedup logic (match on date+amount+description+externalRef).
coreConcepts.Triage: Card-based UI for rapidly reviewing uncategorised transactions: confirm suggestion, change category, mark personal, or skip.

PRD-2.name: AI Categorisation Engine
PRD-2.overview: Claude API-powered automatic classification of financial transactions during import. High-confidence categorisations (≥0.7) are applied automatically; low-confidence items queue for triage.
PRD-2.background: Without AI, every imported transaction must be manually classified — impractical for bulk imports. The engine uses Claude Haiku for cost-efficient batch classification with few-shot learning from user corrections. It adds no new screens; it enhances the import flow and triage UI from PRD-1.
goals: Auto-categorise transactions during import via Claude API
Confidence scores with colour-coded badges (green ≥0.9, amber 0.7-0.89)
AI reasoning explanations accessible via info icon
Re-trigger AI categorisation on demand for uncategorised transactions
Learning from user corrections via few-shot prompting
Graceful degradation when AI is unavailable

coreConcepts.CategorisingJob: Tracks batch AI categorisation operations separately from importJobs. Records batch progress, token usage, and cost.
coreConcepts.ConfidenceThreshold: 0.7 — transactions above this get auto-categorised; below are left uncategorised with suggestion stored for triage.
coreConcepts.FeedbackLoop: aiCategorisationFeedback records capture user overrides to improve future prompts via few-shot examples.

PRD-3.name: Tax Calculation Engine
PRD-3.overview: Core IP of TaxEase — takes categorised transactions, declared reliefs, and entity metadata to produce a complete NTA 2025-compliant tax computation including PIT, CIT, CGT, VAT, WHT credits, and nil return detection.
PRD-3.background: The engine applies Nigeria's six-band progressive PIT system (0% to 25%), rent relief (20%, cap ₦500k), pension/NHIS/NHF reliefs, WHT credit offsets, minimum tax rules, and handles LLC CIT (30% + 4% dev levy) with small company exemption. Accuracy is critical — incorrect calculations expose users to penalties.
goals: Full PIT computation with six progressive bands
Relief calculations (rent, pension, NHIS, NHF, life insurance, mortgage interest)
WHT credit aggregation and offset
CIT for LLCs with small company exemption check
CGT computation for capital disposals
VAT computation (output - input) for VAT-registered entities
Nil return detection and minimum tax rules
Engine versioning for historical immutability

coreConcepts.TaxEngineOutput: Complete computation result including PIT, CIT, CGT, VAT, totalTaxPayable, uncategorisedCount, effectiveTaxRate.
coreConcepts.taxYearSummaries: Cached engine output per entity per tax year for fast dashboard reads.
coreConcepts.TaxDeclarations: User-declared reliefs (rent, pension, etc.) stored per entity per tax year.

PRD-4.name: Invoicing & Client Management
PRD-4.overview: Invoice creation, sending via email with PDF, payment tracking, and the invoice-to-transaction bridge that auto-creates income transactions with WHT fields when an invoice is marked paid.
PRD-4.background: Freelancers and SMEs create invoices with line items, WHT rates (0/5/10%), and optional VAT. Paid invoices automatically generate properly categorised income transactions. Clients are managed in a directory with default currency and WHT rate preferences.
goals: Invoice CRUD with line items, WHT, and VAT calculation
PDF generation via NestJS service and email sending via Resend
Mark-as-paid creates income transaction with WHT fields (invoice-to-transaction bridge)
Recurring invoices on monthly/quarterly schedule
Client directory with CRUD and autocomplete
Overdue detection via daily scheduled function
Payment webhooks (Paystack, Flutterwave)

coreConcepts.InvoiceToTransactionBridge: When markPaid is called, atomically creates an income transaction with amount=totalDue, whtDeducted=whtAmount, whtRate from invoice, categoryId='Freelance/Client Income'.
coreConcepts.InvoiceNumber: Auto-generated sequential format INV-{YEAR}-{NNNN}, atomically incremented.
coreConcepts.DenormalisedClient: clientName and clientEmail stored on invoice at creation for display when client is deleted.

PRD-5.name: Dashboard & Real-Time Overview
PRD-5.overview: Transforms the empty Dashboard shell into a rich, reactive home screen with tax position summary, quick stats, uncategorised banner, recent transactions, invoice activity, and deadline reminders.
PRD-5.background: A read-only aggregation layer — introduces no new database tables. Reads across transactions, taxYearSummaries, invoices, and entities via Convex live queries for automatic real-time updates.
goals: Tax Position Summary card with estimated liability and effective rate
Quick Stats row (income, expenses, WHT credits, outstanding invoices)
Uncategorised Transactions Banner with triage CTA
Recent Transactions list (last 5)
Invoice Activity card
Deadline countdown widget with colour transitions
Compliance reminder chips
Entity and tax year switching with instant refresh

PRD-6.name: Filing Module & Self-Assessment
PRD-6.overview: The culmination of TaxEase — guided pre-filing checklist, tax figure review, immutable self-assessment PDF generation, TaxPro Max submission guide, and payment receipt/TCC upload.
PRD-6.background: The filing record captures a complete TaxEngineOutput snapshot at generation time, creating an immutable audit record. The module walks users through checklist → review → generate → submit → pay → TCC. Nil returns follow the same flow with payment steps skipped.
goals: Filing checklist with grouped items and readiness meter (gate at ≥90%)
Pre-filing review with condensed tax summary and flagged issues
Self-assessment generation with immutable snapshot + NRS-format PDF
5-step TaxPro Max submission guide with deep links
Payment receipt and TCC document upload
Filing status lifecycle: draft → generated → submitted → payment_confirmed → tcc_obtained
Filing history for past tax years

coreConcepts.TaxSummarySnapshot: JSON.stringify(TaxEngineOutput) captured at filing time — immutable audit record that is never retroactively altered.
coreConcepts.FilingStatus: Lifecycle: draft → generated → submitted → payment_pending → payment_confirmed → tcc_obtained. Nil returns skip payment steps.

PRD-7.name: Reports & Export
PRD-7.overview: Analytics layer with Income, Expenses, and Year-on-Year tabs featuring interactive charts, date range filtering, and CSV/PDF export capabilities.
PRD-7.background: Reads exclusively from existing tables (transactions, taxYearSummaries, categories). Introduces no new database tables. Charts include bar (income), doughnut (expenses), and dual-line (year-on-year). PDF export via NestJS service; CSV generated in Convex action.
goals: Three-tab reports screen (Income, Expenses, Year-on-Year)
Date range selector (This Year, Last Year, Custom)
Monthly income bar chart with tap tooltips
Expense doughnut chart by category
Year-on-Year comparison with dual-line chart
CSV export with proper columns per tab
PDF export via NestJS service with professional layout

PRD-8.name: Bank Linking & Live Sync
PRD-8.overview: Upgrades from manual PDF/CSV import to live bank connections via Mono/Stitch (Nigerian Open Banking) and payment platform integrations (Paystack, Flutterwave, Payoneer, Wise).
PRD-8.background: Layer 4 enhancement — additive, not required. Reuses PRD-1's batchUpsert pipeline. Tokens are AES-256-GCM encrypted. Supports manual sync, 6-hour scheduled sync, and webhook-triggered sync. Nigerian Open Banking is still maturing so defensive coding is critical.
goals: Bank linking via Mono Connect and Stitch Link widgets
Paystack/Flutterwave connection via API key
Payoneer/Wise connection via OAuth
AES-256-GCM token encryption
Manual sync, scheduled sync (6h cron), and webhook-triggered sync
Automatic token refresh and re-authentication prompts
Account disconnect/reconnect with transaction preservation
Activate Onboarding Step 4 placeholders

PRD-9.name: Notifications & Reminders
PRD-9.overview: Notification infrastructure with in-app notification centre, push notifications (FCM/APNs), and 7 scheduled cron jobs for filing deadlines, VAT returns, uncategorised alerts, invoice overdue, import results, sync errors, and recurring invoice generation.
PRD-9.background: Cross-cutting enhancement that transforms TaxEase into a year-round companion. Any domain can emit notifications through notifications.create internal mutation. Scheduled functions monitor deadlines and events. Push delivery via Firebase Cloud Messaging.
goals: Notifications table with CRUD and unread badge
Notification centre UI with date grouping and deep-link navigation
Notification settings with granular preference controls
7 scheduled cron jobs for all reminder types
Push notification infrastructure (FCM + APNs)
Notification deduplication and cleanup
Penalty amounts in deadline-related messages

Add `entities` table with fields: userId, name, type ('individual'|'business_name'|'llc'), tin, rcNumber, vatRegistered, vatThresholdExceeded, isDefault, taxYearStart

userStories.title: PIT computation engine — income, expenses, reliefs, bands, WHT
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core PIT tax engine that computes gross income, deductions, reliefs, progressive tax bands, WHT credits, and net payable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create pure function runTaxEngine(input: TaxEngineInput): TaxEngineOutput
Gross income: sum amountNgn where type='income' for entity+taxYear; exclude gifts, loans, transfers
Business expenses: sum amountNgn × (deductiblePercent/100) where type='business_expense' AND isDeductible=true
Assessable profit: grossIncome - totalBusinessExpenses (clamp to 0)
Reliefs: rent (20% of declared, cap ₦500k), pension, NHIS, NHF, life insurance, mortgage interest
Taxable income: assessableProfit - totalReliefs (clamp to 0)
Six PIT bands computed correctly: Band 1 (₦0-800k at 0%), Band 2 (₦800k-2.2m at 15%), Band 3 (₦2.2m-4.2m at 18%), Band 4 (₦4.2m-6.2m at 21%), Band 5 (₦6.2m-56.2m at 23%), Band 6 (₦56.2m+ at 25%)
WHT credits: sum whtDeducted from income transactions; netTaxPayable = max(grossTax - whtCredits, 0)
Minimum tax: if assessableProfit > ₦800k AND grossTax < grossIncome × 0.005, apply max(grossIncome × 0.005, ₦200k)
Engine versioned with TAX_ENGINE_VERSION constant; pure function (same inputs = same outputs)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-3: See '§5.1 PIT Pipeline', '§5.2 PIT Bands', '§5.3 Reliefs', '§5.4 WHT Credits', '§5.5 Minimum Tax' for exact rules

acceptanceCriteria: CIT computation for LLCs: small company exemption if turnover ≤₦100m AND grossFixedAssets ≤₦250m; otherwise CIT=30% of assessableProfit + 4% development levy
CGT: for individuals, gains added to gross income for PIT; for LLCs, 30% flat; exemptions for principal residence, qualifying shares, spouse transfers
VAT: outputVat from invoices (subtotal × 7.5%), inputVat from business purchases (amountNgn × 7.5/107.5 where isVatInclusive), netVatPayable = output - input
Nil return detection: isNilReturn when netTaxPayable === 0 OR taxableIncome ≤ ₦800k
Create tax.getSummary query: runs engine inline, reactive — re-runs when transactions/declarations change
Create tax.refreshSummaryCache mutation: writes to taxYearSummaries for fast dashboard reads

Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Filing schema, checklist backend, and lifecycle mutations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the filingRecords table, checklist computation, and all filing lifecycle mutations.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `filingRecords` table: entityId, userId, taxYear, status (draft/generated/submitted/payment_pending/payment_confirmed/tcc_obtained), selfAssessmentPdfId, paymentReceiptId, tccDocumentId, submittedAt, netTaxPayable, taxSummarySnapshot (string), generatedAt, engineVersion, isNilReturn; indexes by_entityId_taxYear, by_userId, by_status

Create tax.getFilingChecklist query: computes status for 10 checklist items (NIN, entity type, bank accounts, foreign income, income reviewed, categorisation, expenses verified, rent declared, invoices matched, WHT); returns readinessPercent + grouped items
Create filing.initiate mutation: creates/retrieves filing record in 'draft' status (one per entity per year)
Create filing.markSubmitted, filing.uploadPaymentReceipt, filing.uploadTcc mutations with valid status transition enforcement
Nil return auto-transitions: submitted → payment_confirmed (skip payment steps)
Status can only advance forward; re-generation resets to 'generated'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-6: See '§7.1 filingRecords Table Schema' and '§5.1-5.2 Checklist Logic'

userStories.title: Filing checklist UI and pre-filing review screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a guided filing checklist with readiness meter and a condensed pre-filing review of my tax figures.
acceptanceCriteria: Filing screen at /app/filing with tax year selector in header
Readiness meter at top (circular or horizontal progress: '78% Ready')
Grouped checklist: Identity & Registration, Income, Expenses & Deductions, Invoices; each item shows ✅ Done / ⚠️ Needs attention (with detail text) / ❌ Missing
Tappable items navigate to relevant screen to fix the issue; chevron indicator
'Start Filing Review' CTA: enabled only when readiness ≥ 90%; disabled state visually distinct
Pre-Filing Review screen: read-only summary sections (Income, Deductions, Taxable Income, Tax Computation bands, Credits, Net Payable); amber callout for flagged issues
Tip card: 'You can still make amendments after generation'
Footer: 'Go Back to Fix Issues' (shown if flags exist) and 'Generate Self-Assessment Form' (primary CTA)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-6: US-601 through US-604 — See '§4.2 Filing Checklist Screen' and '§4.3 Pre-Filing Review'

userStories.title: Self-assessment generation, preview, and PDF download
userStories.description: As a user, I want to generate my official self-assessment with immutable snapshot and preview/download the PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: Create filing.generateSelfAssessment action: snapshots TaxEngineOutput as JSON string, calls NestJS /pdf/self-assessment, stores PDF in Convex Storage, updates filing record to 'generated' with selfAssessmentPdfId, netTaxPayable, isNilReturn, engineVersion, generatedAt
Confirmation dialog before generation; loading state 'Generating your self-assessment…'
Self-Assessment Preview screen: document-style view with taxpayer details, income schedule, deductions & reliefs, tax computation, WHT credits, net amount payable; NIN masked in preview
PDF download: fetch from Convex Storage; filename TaxEase_SelfAssessment_{EntityName}_{TaxYear}.pdf
Re-generation: allowed in 'draft' or 'generated' status only; replaces snapshot and PDF; blocked after 'submitted'
Navigate-away during generation: continues in background; on return shows success or error
Nil return: PDF marked with 'NIL RETURN', all figures shown with ₦0 net payable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-6: US-605/US-606/US-607/US-614 — See '§6.2 generateSelfAssessment contract' and '§4.4 Preview Screen'

userStories.title: Submission guide, receipt/TCC upload, and filing history
userStories.description: As a user, I want step-by-step TaxPro Max submission instructions and the ability to upload payment proof.
acceptanceCriteria: Submission Guide screen: vertical stepper with 5 collapsible steps: (1) Submit via TaxPro Max with deep link, (2) Make Payment with account details + copy-to-clipboard, (3) Upload Payment Receipt, (4) Obtain TCC, (5) Store Records
'Mark as Submitted' in Step 1: confirmation → status='submitted', submittedAt recorded; nil return auto-advances to payment_confirmed
Upload Payment Receipt in Step 3: file picker (JPG/PNG/PDF, max 10MB), upload progress, stores in Convex Storage, sets paymentReceiptId; advances to payment_confirmed
Upload TCC in Step 4: same upload flow; sets tccDocumentId; advances to tcc_obtained (terminal); completion celebration
Nil return: Step 2 shows 'No payment required'; Step 3 hidden
Filing History: tax year selector shows past filing records with status badges; tapping opens read-only snapshot preview
Status badges colour-coded: draft (neutral), generated (accent), submitted (warning), payment_confirmed (success), tcc_obtained (success with checkmark)
Error states with user-friendly messages and retry for all upload/generation failures
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-6: US-608 through US-618 — See '§4.5 Submission Guide Screen' for stepper layout

userStories.title: Report backend queries and export actions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the report queries and CSV/PDF export actions for Income, Expenses, and Year-on-Year tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create reports.getIncome query: totalIncome, foreignIncome, averageMonthlyIncome, monthlyBreakdown[], categoryBreakdown[] with percentages; filtered by entityId + dateRange
Create reports.getExpenses query: totalExpenses, deductibleExpenses, nonDeductibleExpenses, categoryBreakdown[] (with deductible flags), monthlyBreakdown[]
Create reports.getYearOnYear query: current/prior year metrics from taxYearSummaries, percentage changes, monthly income overlay for both years
Create reports.exportCsv action: generates CSV with proper columns per tab (see PRD-7 US-714/715/716), UTF-8 with BOM, ISO 8601 dates
Create reports.exportPdf action: calls NestJS /pdf/report with structured JSON; stores PDF in Convex Storage; returns download URL
All queries use indexed reads (by_entityId_date, by_entityId_taxYear); entity ownership validated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-7: See '§6 API Requirements' for all query/export contracts

userStories.title: Reports screen — Income and Expenses tabs with charts
userStories.description: As a user, I want Income and Expenses report tabs with summary figures, charts, and category breakdowns.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports screen at /app/reports with three tabs: Income | Expenses | Year-on-Year; Income default
Date range selector: segmented control (This Year | Last Year | Custom); custom opens date picker with start/end
Income tab: 3 summary cards (Total Income, Foreign Income, Avg Monthly Income), monthly bar chart (primary green), income by category breakdown list sorted by amount
Expenses tab: 3 summary cards (Total Expenses, Deductible, Non-Deductible), doughnut chart by category (centre shows total), expense category list with deductible badges
Chart interactions: tap bar/segment for tooltip with exact value; doughnut segments <3% grouped into 'Other'
Empty states per tab: illustration + 'No [income/expense] data for this period' + 'Import Transactions' CTA
Skeleton loading states matching content dimensions; date range persists across tab switches
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-7: US-701 through US-708 — See '§4 UI Specifications' for chart specs and design tokens

userStories.title: Reports — Year-on-Year tab and CSV/PDF export
userStories.description: As a user, I want year-on-year comparison and the ability to export reports as CSV or PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: Year-on-Year tab: two-column comparison card (current vs prior year) with metrics: Total Income, Total Expenses, Tax Liability, Effective Tax Rate; change indicators (↑/↓ with percentage)
Dual-line chart: current year solid primary, prior year dashed accent; monthly income overlay
Prior year missing: '—' for values, 'N/A' for changes, single line on chart, info message
Export floating button (FAB on mobile, header button on web): opens bottom sheet with 'Download as CSV' and 'Download as PDF'
CSV export: loading state during generation; success toast; file opens in share sheet (mobile) or downloads (web)
PDF export: loading state 'Generating PDF…'; calls NestJS service; success toast; professional layout with TaxEase branding
Export filenames: taxease_{tab}_{dates}.csv/.pdf; entity name in PDF header
Error handling: toast with retry on failure; 30s timeout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-7: US-709 through US-720 — See '§4 UI Specifications' for chart and export specs

userStories.title: Bank linking schema, encryption, and OAuth state management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the schema updates, token encryption helpers, and OAuth state management for bank linking.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `metadata` field to connectedAccounts: institutionId, institutionLogo, accountType, accountNumber, linkProvider (mono/stitch), apiKeyHash
Add indexes: connectedAccounts.by_providerAccountId (webhook lookup), by_status (scheduled sync)
Add `oauthStates` table: userId, entityId, provider, stateToken, redirectUri, expiresAt; indexes by_stateToken, by_expiresAt
Create lib/encryption.ts: AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt functions using ENCRYPTION_KEY env var; 12-byte random IV prepended to ciphertext
Create accounts.initiateOAuth mutation: creates oauthStates entry with random UUID state token and 10-minute TTL
Create oauthStates.validate mutation: looks up by stateToken, verifies not expired, returns data, deletes entry (one-time use)
Scheduled cleanup: delete expired oauthStates entries hourly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-8: See '§7.2 connectedAccounts Enhanced Schema', '§7.3 oauthStates', 'Appendix A Security'

userStories.title: Bank and payment platform connection flows
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the OAuth and API key connection actions for all supported providers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create accounts.handleOAuthCallback action: validates state token, exchanges auth code for tokens via provider API, encrypts tokens (AES-256-GCM), fetches account details, creates/updates connectedAccounts, triggers initial syncNow
Create accounts.addApiKeyAccount action: validates Paystack key via /balance endpoint (or Flutterwave /balances), encrypts key, stores SHA-256 hash in metadata.apiKeyHash, creates account
Provider client modules: lib/providers/mono.ts, stitch.ts, paystack.ts, flutterwave.ts, payoneer.ts, wise.ts — each with exchangeCode, getAccountDetails, getTransactions functions
OAuth error handling: user cancel → no account created; provider error → toast with retry
API key validation failure: 'Invalid API key. Please check and try again.'
Webhook endpoints: POST /webhooks/oauth-callback (returns HTML for WebView communication), POST /webhooks/bank-notification (verifies HMAC signature, triggers sync)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-8: US-804 through US-809 — See '§6.3 Connected Accounts Actions' and 'Appendix B Provider Matrix'

userStories.title: Sync pipeline — manual, scheduled, and webhook-triggered
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sync pipeline that fetches transactions from providers and feeds them through batchUpsert.
acceptanceCriteria: Create accounts.syncNow action: reads account → decrypts tokens → checks expiry/refreshes → calls provider API for transactions since lastSyncedAt → creates importJob → parses response via provider transformer → calls batchUpsert → updates lastSyncedAt

Provider transformer (lib/providers/transformer.ts): normalises provider-specific responses to canonical transaction shape; handles Mono/Paystack kobo amounts (÷100), Flutterwave currency units, Wise multi-currency
Create accounts.scheduledSync cron (every 6 hours): queries active accounts with tokens (excludes manual/statement_upload), stagger syncs to respect rate limits
Webhook-triggered sync: POST /webhooks/bank-notification verifies HMAC-SHA256, looks up account by providerAccountId, triggers syncNow; reauthorization_required sets status='error'
Create accounts.refreshToken action: decrypts refresh token, calls provider refresh endpoint, encrypts new tokens, stores; 5-minute proactive refresh buffer
Rate limiting: max one manual sync per account per 60 seconds
FX conversion for foreign-currency synced transactions using CBN rates (same pipeline as PRD-1)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-8: US-810 through US-823 — See '§6 API Requirements' for all sync function specs

userStories.title: Connected accounts UI and onboarding Step 4 activation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage connected accounts and have real bank linking options in onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: Connected Accounts screen: list of account cards (institution logo, name, type badge, last synced, status badge Active/Error/Disconnected); sorted active first

Account detail screen: full info card, sync status, 'Sync Now' button (disabled during sync), last sync result (imported/duplicates/status), sync history (last 5 jobs), error banner with 'Retry'/'Re-authenticate', 'Disconnect' in danger zone
Add Account screen: method cards (Connect Bank, Connect Paystack, Connect Flutterwave, Connect Payoneer, Connect Wise, Upload Statement)
API Key form for Paystack/Flutterwave: account name, masked secret key input, security callout, 'Connect' button with loading validation
Disconnect: confirmation dialog, clears tokens, sets status='disconnected', preserves transactions
Reconnect: same OAuth/API flow targeting existing document; providerAccountId must match
Onboarding Step 4: previously placeholder cards now navigate to real linking flows; after link, return to Step 4 with checkmark
Empty state: 'No accounts linked yet' + 'Link an Account' CTA + 'Or upload a bank statement' secondary link
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-8: US-801 through US-824 — See '§4.2 Connected Accounts Screen' through '§4.8 Disconnect Dialog'

userStories.title: Notifications schema, CRUD backend, and push tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the notifications infrastructure — tables, CRUD, push tokens, and the internal create mutation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `notifications` table: userId, type (8 notification types), title, body, entityId, relatedId, read (boolean), readAt; indexes by_userId_read, by_userId_creationTime
Add `pushTokens` table: userId, token, platform (ios/android/web), active, lastUsedAt; indexes by_userId_active, by_token
Create notifications.list query: paginated (default 50), ordered by _creationTime desc
Create notifications.getUnreadCount query: count where userId + read=false
Create notifications.markRead and notifications.markAllRead mutations
Create notifications.create internal mutation: inserts notification, checks pushEnabled, schedules push.send if enabled
Create pushTokens.register mutation: upserts by token string
Create push.send internal action: queries active tokens, dispatches via Firebase Admin SDK, deactivates invalid tokens on NotRegistered error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-9: See '§7 Data Models' for table schemas and '§6 API Requirements' for function specs

userStories.title: Scheduled notification functions — deadlines, alerts, and reminders
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all 7 scheduled cron jobs that monitor deadlines and events and emit notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Create reminders.checkFilingDeadline (daily 08:00 WAT): checks daysUntilDeadline against each user's deadlineReminderDays; creates notification per entity with penalty-aware message templates
Create reminders.checkVatDeadline (daily 08:00 WAT): for users with vatReminderEnabled and VAT-registered entities; reminds at 7, 3, 1 days before 21st
Create reminders.uncategorisedAlert (daily 10:00 WAT): for users with frequency='daily'; counts uncategorised transactions per entity
Create reminders.uncategorisedAlertWeekly (Mondays 10:00 WAT): same for frequency='weekly'
Notification deduplication: check for existing notification of same type+user+day before creating
All message templates include penalty amounts for deadline-related notifications (₦100k filing, ₦50k/month, ₦50k VAT)
Create notifications.cleanup (weekly Sundays 02:00 WAT): delete read >90 days, unread >180 days, batch of 100
Register all crons in convex/crons.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-9: US-915 through US-923, US-929/US-930 — See '§6.4 Scheduled Functions' for cron specs

userStories.title: Notification centre UI, bell badge, and settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want a notification centre with date grouping, bell badge, and granular notification preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification bell icon in header on all authenticated screens with red unread badge (max '99+'); real-time reactive via getUnreadCount
Notification centre screen: chronological list grouped by date (Today, Yesterday, This Week, Earlier); unread cards have primary-light background + coloured left border
Each card: type icon (colour-coded), title, body (truncated 2 lines), relative timestamp; tapping marks read and navigates to related screen
'Mark all as read' button in header; swipe-to-mark-read on mobile
Empty state: bell+checkmark illustration, 'You're all caught up!'

Notification Settings screen (Settings → Notifications): grouped toggles for filing deadlines (with day chips 30/14/7/1), VAT reminders, uncategorised alerts (daily/weekly), invoice overdue (1/3/7 days), push notifications (with device permission status)
Push toggle: triggers OS permission request on first enable; 'Enable in Settings' deep link when denied
Save preferences via updatePreferences mutation with success toast
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-9: US-901 through US-914 — See '§4 UI Specifications' for notification centre and settings layouts
